title: Sweet Sticky Malaysian Chicken Bao
description: A kid-friendly Malaysian dish featuring caramelized chicken and long-fermentation-dough, with anti-inflammatory friendly ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, honey, ginger, and garlic to make the marinade.
Add the chicken to the marinade and mix well to coat.
Let the chicken marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Roll out the long fermentation dough to a thickness of about 1/4 inch.
Spoon about 1 tablespoon of caramel sauce onto the center of each dough circle.
Place a few pieces of the marinated chicken onto the caramel sauce.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges to seal.
Brush the tops of the bao with sesame oil.
Bake the bao for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
